title: Direct Hire To Fight Fires
plain_summary: This bill makes it easier for the government to hire wildland firefighters by letting them skip the usual slow hiring process. It gives the Department of Agriculture and Department of the Interior special power to hire firefighters and support workers quickly. The bill also requires these agencies to create better policies for hiring and keeping firefighters, and to make it easier for firefighters to move between different government agencies. Every year, the agencies must tell Congress how many firefighters they need and report this information on a public website.
last_action_text: Reported by the Committee on Natural Resources. H. Rept. 119-432, Part I.
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Direct Hire To Fight Fires Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Direct Hire Authority to Appoint Individuals to Federal Wildland Firefighting and Firefighting Support Positions in the Forest Service or the Department of the Interior
sections.text: This section changes federal law to give the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior special power to hire firefighters quickly. They can skip the normal government hiring rules when they hire people for wildland firefighting jobs or jobs that support firefighting. This applies to jobs in the Forest Service and the Department of the Interior. The jobs this covers include Forestry Technicians, people who operate aircraft, people in administration jobs that support firefighting and qualify for firefighter retirement benefits, people in natural resources and biological science jobs that support firefighting and qualify for firefighter retirement benefits, Aviation Management Specialists, Equipment Operators who support firefighting and qualify for firefighter retirement benefits, Dispatchers who support firefighting and qualify for firefighter retirement benefits, Equipment Specialists, and any new job categories created specifically for wildland firefighting. The law also adds this new section to the official table of contents in the federal code.

sections.title: Section 3 Streamlining and Transparency in Federal Wildland Firefighting Hiring at the Forest Service and the Department of the Interior
sections.text: Within one year after this law passes, the Secretary of Agriculture and Secretary of the Interior must work with the Director of the Office of Personnel Management to create new hiring policies. These policies must make it faster to hire wildland firefighters and support staff, remove duplicate steps in the hiring process, make it easier to rehire people who worked for these agencies before, and remove barriers that make it hard for firefighters to move between agencies. Starting one year after this law passes and every year after that, both Secretaries must send a report to Congress by February first. The report must include how many wildland firefighters and support staff the agency thinks it will need that year broken down by job type, how many hiring events the agency plans to hold, how many wildland firefighters and support staff currently work for the agency broken down by job type, how many wildland firefighting job openings exist in each state, any problems the agency faces in meeting its hiring goals, how the agency is using the new direct hiring power, how the agency is using the new streamlined hiring policies, and suggestions to Congress for better ways to recruit and keep wildland firefighters and support staff. Each Secretary must also publish this report on a public website every year. In this section, agency means the Forest Service for National Forest lands and the Department of the Interior for lands under the Secretary of the Interior. Relevant Congressional Committees means the House Committees on Oversight and Government Reform, Agriculture, and Natural Resources, and the Senate Committees on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs, Agriculture Nutrition and Forestry, and Energy and Natural Resources. Secretaries means the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior.
